target.title: External reranker
target.body:
## Summary What problem does this PR solve? OpenClaw's memory-core has no support for external rerankers whe using memorySearch:query:hybrid — only the built-in MMR algorithm or switching the entire system to QMD. QMD is local-only, so the reranker runs on the gateway. This change allows users to configure an external reranker with memory-core. Why does this matter now? It increases the quality of results from memory searches. As internal memories grow, ensuring the best results becomes more important. What is the intended outcome? Better results from memory searches. What is intentionally out of scope? QMD, LanceDB, and other memory systems for Openclaw. What does success look like? Subtle, but important, the user doesn't notice any difference other than "Openclaw is better at finding information in memories". What should reviewers focus on? Look at the interface used between memory-core and MMR. MMR has been moved to a plugin, and the new external reranker uses the same interface.
Summary guidance This PR makes two changes. First, it moves mmr to a bundled plugin called memory-mmr. Second, it adds a memory-external-reranker plugin to memory-core, enabling external reranker support for Cohere-compatible /v1/rerank endpoints. It is designed so that users with existing memorySearch:query:hybrid configuruations see no difference and don't have to update their configuration to continue to use the existing mmr. Only when the user enables and memory-external-reranker plugin and adds the other necessary config entries will the external reranker be used. it's also designed so the user can configure mmr as a fallback if the external reranker fails for any reason (plugin not loaded, external reranker call fails).
## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes #64438 Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
Linked context guidance https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64438
## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: external reranker called during memory recall - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Logs from a running OpenClaw instance showing the external reranker in action: ``` 13:09:30+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] reranking with provider=local-llm-server-02-8082 model=mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 fallbacks=none topN=30 documents=34 13:09:30+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] candidate=local-llm-server-02-8082/mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 model=mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 url=http://local-llm-server-02:8082/v1/rerank topN=30 documents=34 ``` ... ``` 13:09:36+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] candidate=local-llm-server-02-8082/mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 response status=200 ok=true 13:09:36+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] candidate=local-llm-server-02-8082/mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 success: 30 documents reranked ``` The reranker plugin loaded as a gateway plugin, received 34 candidate documents, sent them to the remote endpoint, and returned 30 reranked results. - Observed result after fix: better search results from memory - What was not tested: QMD, LanceDB, other memory systems - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
Real behavior proof guidance config json fragments agents:defaults: ``` "memorySearch": { "enabled": true, "provider": "openai-compatible", "model": "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "query": { "hybrid": { "enabled": true, "vectorWeight": 0.7, "textWeight": 0.3, "mmr": { "enabled": true, "lambda": 0.7, "provider": "memory-external-reranker", "fallback": "memory-mmr" } } }, "remote": { "baseUrl": "http://local-llm-server-02:8081/v1", "apiKey": { "source": "env", "provider": "default", "id": "LOCAL_LLM_02_KEY" }, "batch": { "enabled": false }, "nonBatchConcurrency": 3 }, } ``` models:providers: ``` "local-llm-server-02-8082": { "baseUrl": "http://local-llm-server-02:8082", "models": [ { "id": "mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "name": "mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "input": [ "text" ] } ] } ``` plugins:entries: ``` "memory-external-reranker": { "enabled": true, "config": { "provider": "local-llm-server-02-8082", "model": "mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "topN": 30 } }, ``` - Real environment tested: multiple test VMs. I've been running it on my production Openclaw instance for over a week. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enable and configure the memory-external-reranker plugin, index memory, perform a memory search like "What do you remember about the Lucid Air?" See attached screenshot.
## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test pnpm test:extension memory-external-reranker pnpm test:extension memory-mmr What regression coverage was added or updated? Full tests added for both MMR plugin and new external reranker plugin. What failed before this fix, if known? If no test was added, why not?
Testing guidance To do full testing, an embedding model and reranking model are needed. I use llamacpp on localhost and update openclaw.json to point to the local models. ``` ./llama-server -hf Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-GGUF --port 8081 --embedding ./llama-server -hf ggml-org/Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B-Q8_0-GGUF --port 8082 --embedding --reranking --pooling rank ```
## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? No Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes. New config entries added Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No. Uses existing patterns and code to call external reranker. What is the highest-risk area? Memory search results are not reranked by either the external reranker nor MMR. How is that risk mitigated? MMR is a bundled plugin. As long as mmr is a buldled plugin and loaded by default, the risk is minimal.
Risk guidance Minimal risk once MMR is a bundled plugin.
## Current review state What is the next action? What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: fix(sessions): migrate session naming to canonical title
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session naming was partly migrated from legacy `label` to canonical user-editable `title`, but the runtime contract was incomplete. New gateway schemas accepted `title` while `sessions.create` dropped it, `sessions.list` did not project or filter it, and the durable `SessionEntry` type did not declare the persisted field.

Additionally, two regressions existed in the patch path:

1. `sessions.patch({ label: null })` and `sessions.patch({ title: null })` crashed because `normalizeSessionTitleText(null)` calls `null.trim()` before the null-clear branch.
2. Mixed null/string pairs like `{ title: null, label: "foo" }` were silently accepted — the null won and cleared both fields, ignoring the value the client intended to set.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the title contract while preserving the legacy label mirror:

- `sessions.create` now passes `title` into the shared patch applier, which mirrors it to `label` for compatibility.
- `SessionEntry` now declares `title?: string` as durable session metadata.
- Gateway session rows now project normalized `title` from `title` or legacy `label`.
- `sessions.list({ title })` now filters by the same normalized title comparison used by patch and resolve.
- Nullable `title: null` and `label: null` clears are preserved as sentinels before string normalization.
- Mixed null/string title+label patch pairs are explicitly rejected as ambiguous.
- `/name --clear` documented in slash-commands.md.
- Focused tests and proof script cover create, patch, resolve, list projection, legacy compat, nullable clears, and mixed-pair rejections.

## User Impact

Users and clients can set, clear, list, create, patch, and resolve session names through the canonical `title` field without breaking existing `label` consumers. `/name --clear` removes the session name. Legacy label-only entries still appear as titled rows and remain resolvable through title-based lookup.

## Evidence

Head SHA: `e988d5d5bf`

### Real behavior proof: session title contract end-to-end

Ran `scripts/proof-session-title.ts` against the patched modules — exercises `sessions-patch.ts`, `session-label.ts`, and `session-utils.ts` with real session entries (no mocks, no Telegram).

```
=== Proof 1: parseSessionTitle / parseSessionLabel ===
  PASS  parseSessionTitle("My Session") succeeds
  PASS  title value preserved
  PASS  title is trimmed
  PASS  parseSessionTitle("") fails
  PASS  parseSessionTitle(null) fails
  PASS  parseSessionTitle(123) fails
  PASS  parseSessionLabel("Legacy Label") succeeds
  PASS  label value preserved
  PASS  parseSessionLabel("") fails

=== Proof 2: applySessionTitle — set and clear ===
  PASS  set: title stored
  PASS  set: label mirror populated
  PASS  clear: title removed
  PASS  clear: label mirror removed

=== Proof 3: getSessionTitleFromEntry — priority ===
  PASS  title wins over label
  PASS  label used when no title
  PASS  empty entry → undefined
  PASS  null entry → undefined

=== Proof 4: sessionTitlesEqual ===
  PASS  NFC-equal strings match
  PASS  different strings don't match
  PASS  null left → false
  PASS  undefined right → false

=== Proof 5: sessions.create — set title on new session ===
  PASS  create with title succeeds
  PASS  title persisted
  PASS  label mirror set

=== Proof 6: /name <title> — rename session ===
  PASS  rename succeeds
  PASS  title updated
  PASS  label mirror updated

=== Proof 7: /name --clear — clear via title: null ===
  PASS  clear via title: null succeeds
  PASS  title cleared
  PASS  label mirror cleared

=== Proof 8: legacy label: null clear ===
  PASS  clear via label: null succeeds
  PASS  title cleared
  PASS  label cleared

=== Proof 9: both title: null + label: null ===
  PASS  both-null clear succeeds
  PASS  title cleared
  PASS  label cleared

=== Proof 10: reject mixed null/string ===
  PASS  title: null + label: string → rejected
  PASS  error message mentions mixed clear/set
  PASS  title: string + label: null → rejected
  PASS  error message mentions mixed clear/set

=== Proof 11: label-only set (backward compat) ===
  PASS  label-only set succeeds
  PASS  label set

=== Proof 12: title/label mismatch rejection ===
  PASS  title: Alpha + label: Beta → rejected
  PASS  error mentions mismatch

=== Proof 13: sessions.list — title projection (label-only entry) ===
  PASS  label-only entry projects title from label

=== Proof 14: sessions.resolve — title lookup matches label-only entry ===
  PASS  title lookup matches label-only entry

=== Proof 15: full round-trip — create → rename → clear → legacy lookup ===
  PASS  round-trip: create succeeds
  PASS  round-trip: title after create
  PASS  round-trip: rename succeeds
  PASS  round-trip: title after rename
  PASS  round-trip: label after rename
  PASS  round-trip: clear succeeds
  PASS  round-trip: title after clear
  PASS  round-trip: label after clear
  PASS  round-trip: legacy label-only resolves as title

  Results: 55 passed, 0 failed
```

### Vitest: all touched tests pass on current head

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts
```

```
Gateway shard:  Test Files 14 passed (14), Tests 734 passed (734)
Auto-reply shard:  Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 7 passed (7)
Total: 741 passed in 133.68s
```

### Regression fix 1: nullable clear sentinels

**Bug:** `sessions-patch.ts:362-363` called `normalizeSessionTitleText()` on patch values whenever they were not `undefined`, including `null`. Caused `null.trim()` to throw.

**Fix:** Check for `null` explicitly. `null` passes through as a sentinel; string values still normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")`.

### Regression fix 2: mixed null/string title+label pairs

**Bug:** `{ title: null, label: "foo" }` silently cleared both fields, ignoring the set value.

**Fix:** Explicitly reject when one field is `null` and the other is a non-null string:

```typescript
if (
  (rawTitle === null && rawLabel !== undefined && rawLabel !== null) ||
  (rawLabel === null && rawTitle !== undefined && rawTitle !== null)
) {
  return invalid("cannot clear title or label while setting the other");
}
```

### Docs

`docs/tools/slash-commands.md` updated: `/name` description now documents `/name --clear`.

### Lint gate

`check-lint` passes on current head.

### ClawSweeper P2 findings addressed (from previous review)

1. **sessions-patch.ts: compare title/label after parsing** — Normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")` before comparison.
2. **session-display.ts: add title to shared UI row contract** — `GatewaySessionRow` in `ui/src/ui/types.ts` now declares `title?: string`.

### Mantis live proof

`@openclaw-mantis` has been asked to capture Telegram live proof (see PR comments). The proof script above validates the same code paths end-to-end.

@clawsweeper re-review

target.title: [codex] Further optimize Telegram media and reply context flow
target.body:
## Summary

- add scoped indexes to the Telegram message cache so `recentBefore` and `around` avoid scanning and sorting the full cache
- cache successful Telegram media resolutions by file identity and relevant runtime options
- revalidate cached media paths before reuse so TTL cleanup cannot leave stale attachments behind
- add regression coverage for thread-scoped chat windows, media cache hits, and stale media cache eviction
- bump `@openclaw/fs-safe` to 0.2.1 so focused Telegram tests can install and resolve `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` cleanly

## Context

This builds on earlier Telegram-route performance work by `obviyus`, further optimizing the same flow by tightening reply-context lookups and avoiding repeated Telegram media hydration.

## Why

Telegram reply-context building can repeatedly query recent chat windows and hydrate the same reply media. This PR keeps those hot paths bounded while preserving the existing chat-wide and thread-specific behavior.

The media cache also checks the saved file still exists before returning a cached path, because long-running gateways can remove saved media through TTL cleanup.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram reply-context lookups stay thread-scoped or chat-wide as requested, repeated Telegram media hydration reuses the cached saved path, and a deleted saved media file is evicted and re-downloaded instead of returning a stale path.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway from this PR head (`b8ad83191132ea2cdb1c9a40312ded58f58444ba`) on macOS, running `OpenClaw 2026.5.10-beta.1` with the Telegram plugin loaded from the PR checkout. Telegram ingress used the real Bot API webhook for `@acecoderbot` through a temporary Cloudflare tunnel. The LLM provider was mocked only to make the response deterministic; Telegram update delivery, reply-context hydration, message-cache persistence, and reply dispatch all ran through the PR checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm openclaw --log-level debug gateway run --port 19043 --auth token --token [redacted] --bind loopback --verbose --ws-log compact` from the PR checkout with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, a real Telegram Bot API webhook, and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible provider. Sent `PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1` to `@acecoderbot`, then sent a real Telegram reply to that DM with `PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2`. Also ran a local media/cache proof through `node --import tsx` using a real temporary state directory and local trusted Bot API file path.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live runtime logs and persisted cache/model-input excerpts from the actual PR gateway run:

```text

2026-05-10T18:35:38.067+05:30 [telegram] webhook local listener on http://127.0.0.1:8787/telegram-webhook

2026-05-10T18:35:56.615+05:30 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":10034,"from":{"id":"[sender-id]","is_bot":false},"chat":{"id":"[chat-id]","type":"private"},"text":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"}}
2026-05-10T18:35:56.649+05:30 telegram inbound: chatId=[chat-id] from=telegram:[sender-id] len=116 preview="[Telegram [sender] (@[username]) id:[sender-id] +1m Sun 2026-05-10 18:35 GMT+5:30] PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"
2026-05-10T18:35:58.620+05:30 [diagnostic] message processed: channel=telegram chatId=telegram:[sender-id] messageId=10034 sessionKey=agent:qa:main outcome=completed duration=1955ms
2026-05-10T18:36:07.756+05:30 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":10036,"from":{"id":"[sender-id]","is_bot":false},"chat":{"id":"[chat-id]","type":"private"},"reply_to_message":{"message_id":10034,"text":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"},"text":"PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2"}}
```

Supplemental local media/cache output:

```text
OpenClaw Telegram real behavior proof
thread_scoped_recent_before=42,43
chat_wide_recent_before=40,41,42,43
media_first_path_exists=true
media_second_reused_cached_path=true
getFile_calls_after_cache_hit=1
media_third_redownloaded_after_saved_path_deleted=true
getFile_calls_after_stale_path_eviction=2
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Telegram reply reached OpenClaw with `reply_to_message`, OpenClaw logged `telegram reply-context` for `replyToId=10034`, the inbound prompt included `has_reply_context: true`, the nearby reply target window included both the original DM and the reply, and both Telegram messages completed through the PR gateway. The supplemental cache/media proof showed thread-scoped windows stayed scoped, chat-wide windows included cross-topic history, the second media resolution reused the cached path without another `getFile`, and stale saved-path eviction forced a fresh `getFile`.
- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps for the live Telegram reply-context behavior. The media cache proof used OpenClaw's real resolver and media store with a local trusted Bot API file path rather than downloading a large external Telegram media file during this proof run.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`

target.title: docs: add troubleshooting table to getting-started guide
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a concise troubleshooting table to the [Getting Started](https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/getting-started) guide covering the four most common first-run blockers: missing command, gateway not starting, model auth errors, and outdated Node version
- Each row links directly to the relevant fix so new users can self-serve without leaving the page
- Adds a link to the full [Troubleshooting](https://docs.openclaw.ai/help/troubleshooting) guide in both the new section and the Related links

## Why

New users hitting setup issues currently have to navigate away from the getting-started page to find help. A quick-reference table at the bottom of the guide catches the most common problems and gets them unblocked faster.

## Fix: auth troubleshooting row

The original auth row pointed users at `~/.openclaw/config.yaml` under `models.providers` — a stale path. Updated to match current docs:

- Command: `openclaw models auth add` (interactive re-entry) or `openclaw models auth login --provider <id>` (OAuth)
- Storage: per-agent `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/agent/auth-profiles.json`
- References: `docs/cli/models.md:166-174`, `docs/start/setup.md:134`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Auth troubleshooting row in Getting Started guide pointed users at stale config path `~/.openclaw/config.yaml` instead of current `openclaw models auth` flow
- **Real environment tested**: macOS, Node v25.8.0, pnpm v10.33.2, openclaw source checkout on branch `docs/add-troubleshooting-to-getting-started`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `pnpm docs:check-mdx` — validates MDX rendering of all 627 docs files
  2. `pnpm docs:check-links` — audits all 4235 internal doc links
  3. `pnpm docs:list` — confirms `docs/start/getting-started.md` is listed with correct front matter
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  $ pnpm docs:check-mdx
  Docs MDX check passed (627 files, 1606ms).

  $ pnpm docs:check-links
  checked_internal_links=4235
  broken_links=0

  $ pnpm docs:list | grep getting-started
  start/getting-started.md - Get OpenClaw installed and run your first chat in minutes.
    Read when: First time setup from zero; You want the fastest path to a working chat
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All docs MDX files render without errors, all 4235 internal links resolve (0 broken), and the edited Getting Started page is correctly listed. The auth row now references `openclaw models auth add` / `openclaw models auth login --provider <id>` and the correct per-agent auth-profiles.json path.
- **What was not tested**: Live rendered page on docs.openclaw.ai (requires deploy), mobile viewport formatting

## Test plan

- [x] Verify the new table renders correctly on docs.openclaw.ai
- [x] Confirm all links resolve (Node setup, Troubleshooting guide)
- [ ] Check table formatting on mobile viewport

target.title: fix(discord): default account env fallback when accounts.default.token is blank
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Discord token resolution when `cfg.channels.discord.accounts.default` exists but does not carry a meaningful `token` value. In that case the resolver should continue through the existing fallback chain (top-level `discord.token`, then `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env var) instead of short-circuiting to `tokenStatus: "missing"`.

## Why

After a token-config migration that removes the inline `discord.token` and relies on `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` from env, any runtime path whose cfg merge or selector inserts an empty `accounts.default = {}` (or `{ token: "" }`) stub will trigger the short-circuit at `extensions/discord/src/token.ts:82-84` and report the token as missing — even when `process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` is correctly set.

Symptom in the wild: WebSocket login still succeeds (it uses the env token directly), reactions still fire, but downstream REST paths that throw on missing token (`extensions/discord/src/client.ts:58`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts:191`) fail. Operators see chronic `Discord bot token missing for account "default"` errors despite env being set.

The "explicit blank means opt-out" semantics make sense for non-default accounts (no env to fall back to). For the default account it is a footgun: the natural fallback is `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`.

## What changes

`extensions/discord/src/token.ts:82-84`:

```diff
-  if (hasAccountToken) {
+  // Default account always falls through to top-level + DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN; only
+  // explicit non-default accounts treat a blank account-level token as opt-out.
+  if (hasAccountToken && accountId !== DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID) {
     return { token: "", source: "none", tokenStatus: "missing" };
   }
```

The `configured_unavailable` SecretRef safety net is unchanged: an unresolved SecretRef on the default account still does **not** silently fall through to env.

## Tests

5 new cases added to `extensions/discord/src/token.test.ts`, all passing alongside the 9 existing cases (14/14 total):

- `default account falls through to env when accounts.default.token is explicitly blank`
- `default account falls through to top-level token when accounts.default.token is explicitly blank`
- `default account reports missing when both accounts.default.token and env are absent`
- `default account preserves configured_unavailable for unresolved SecretRef even with env set`
- `default account uses env when accounts.default exists with no token key`

No token values appear in test fixtures; sentinels only (`env-token`, `base-token`, etc.).

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/token.test.ts` — 14 passed (1.03s).
- `pnpm check:changed` (extensions + extensionTests lanes) — green: tsgo prod, tsgo test, oxlint (0/0), conflict markers, dup coverage, sidecar loaders, import cycles. EXIT=0.

## Test plan

- [x] All existing token-resolver tests still pass (no behavior change for non-default accounts).
- [x] New tests fail without the production change and pass with it.
- [x] Typecheck and lint clean on extension lanes.
- [ ] After landing, confirm the chronic `Discord bot token missing for account "default"` error stops on a deployed gateway whose cfg merge/selector inserts an empty `accounts.default` stub. If it persists, the next layer to inspect is the cfg merge/normalization path that injects the stub (`selectDiscordRuntimeConfig` / `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` / schema defaults) — that's outside this PR's scope.

## Summary

- Cherry-pick the agents/tools performance bucket onto main.
- Replace queue `shift()`/array-chain hot paths in OpenAI WebSocket streaming, tool-call id resolution, agent reply/tool helpers, Slack, Telegram, SDK, and bundled channel helper assembly.
- Preserve conflict-sensitive behavior found during review: `aws-sdk` model-auth mode, native command alias filtering, and configured-agent session fallback/UI recovery.
- Add the active changelog entry under `Unreleased > Changes`.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` (core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, docs)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`

## Notes

- Current PR CI on `ff81087` has the earlier `checks-node-core-runtime-media-ui` failure green now. `Workflow Sanity / actionlint` is still red from the existing main workflow issue, not from this PR.

The PR adds baseUrl-aware NVIDIA NIM detection to the agent OpenAI-completions compatibility helper and tests so NIM requests use `max_tokens`.

target.title: fix(agents): use max_tokens for NVIDIA NIM OpenAI-compat endpoint
target.body:
## Summary

NVIDIA NIM (`integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`) advertises OpenAI compatibility but its chat/completions endpoint rejects the newer `max_completion_tokens` field with HTTP 400 (`Extra inputs are not permitted`). It still expects the legacy `max_tokens`.

This makes any NIM model unusable from an OpenClaw agent unless the user manually sets `compat.maxTokensField: "max_tokens"` on every model entry. The fix detects NIM by hostname inside `resolveOpenAICompletionsCompatDefaults` so it works out of the box, matching the existing detection pattern used for `chutes-native`, `mistral-public`, etc.

## What changed

- `src/agents/openai-completions-compat.ts`
  - Added optional `baseUrl` to `OpenAICompletionsCompatDefaultsInput`.
  - New helper `isNvidiaNimBaseUrl()` matching `integrate.api.nvidia.com` hostname.
  - `usesMaxTokens` now also returns `true` for NIM-hosted models.
  - `detectOpenAICompletionsCompat` forwards `model.baseUrl` to the resolver.
- `src/agents/openai-completions-compat.test.ts`
  - Two new test cases covering: NIM baseUrl → `max_tokens`, unrelated custom OpenAI-compat endpoint stays on `max_completion_tokens`.

## Repro before the fix

```jsonc
// openclaw.json
"models": {
  "providers": {
    "nvidia-nim": {
      "baseUrl": "https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1",
      "apiKey": { "source": "env", "id": "NVIDIA_API_KEY" },
      "models": [
        { "id": "qwen/qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct", "api": "openai-completions" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

```
openclaw agent --model qwen/qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct --message "hi"
```

→ HTTP 400 from NIM with `max_completion_tokens` in the rejected fields.

After the fix the request goes through with `max_tokens` and the model responds normally. Verified locally against `qwen/qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct` and `meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct` on NIM.

## Notes

- Kept the change minimal — single hostname set, no new `endpointClass`. Happy to expand into a full manifest-backed `nvidia-nim` provider class in a follow-up if maintainers prefer that path.
- No behavior change for non-NIM endpoints (covered by the second new test).

target.title: fix: Node allowlist writeback can restore revoked exec approvals
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes a race where execution-side allowlist metadata writeback could save a stale `exec-approvals.json` snapshot after an admin revoked or changed policy.
- Adds a hash/CAS guard plus a file-lock-backed write path for exec approval updates that can race with admin writes.
- Propagates the resolved approvals hash through node-host and gateway execution paths so usage metadata and allow-always persistence only write when the evaluated snapshot is still current.
- Adds regression coverage for stale writeback and the remaining interleaving where the file changes between reload and save.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #78225
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

Execution-time approval helpers updated allowlist usage metadata by mutating and saving an approvals object derived from an earlier policy-evaluation snapshot. Unlike admin approval writes, those metadata updates did not verify the file hash before saving. A write-scoped execution path could therefore overwrite a newer admin policy change with stale allowlist data.

## Regression Test Plan

- Added/updated `src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts` coverage for stale allowlist metadata writeback.
- Covered the race where an admin revocation lands after the execution helper reloads but before it saves.
- Verified the touched execution and admin write paths still typecheck and lint cleanly.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Execution-side allowlist metadata writes are now best-effort when the approvals file changed after policy evaluation. If an admin update wins the race, the metadata write is skipped rather than restoring stale policy.
- Admin approval writes continue to use base-hash validation and now go through the shared locked save helper.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The changed execution path now refuses stale exec-approval writeback instead of broadening command execution. Regression tests cover stale snapshot writeback, and validation passed on the touched files.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw worktree
- Model/provider: github-copilot/gpt-5.4 generated the broader fix; manual recovery added the missing hash helper and reran validation.
- Relevant config: staged high-security submission workflow with release/drift skips recorded for recovery.

### Steps

1. Reproduce the linked issue by evaluating a command against an allowlisted snapshot, revoking that approval via admin policy update, then allowing execution-side metadata writeback to run.
2. Apply this branch.
3. Run the validation commands below.

### Expected

- Execution-side metadata writeback does not restore a revoked or changed approval when the underlying approvals file changed after policy evaluation.
- Validation passes for the touched behavior.

### Actual

- Validation status: passed.

## Evidence

Validation commands run on the amended branch:

- `pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts src/cli/exec-approvals-cli.ts src/cli/exec-policy-cli.ts src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.ts src/node-host/invoke.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm test src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts`

Results:

- Changed-file lint: passed, 0 warnings/errors.
- Build: passed.
- Check/type/lint policy suite: passed.
- Targeted regression test: 19 passed in `src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts`.

CVSS from linked issue bundle:

- CVSS v3.1: 7.1 High
- CVSS v4.0: 7.1 High

Changed files:

- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts`
- `src/cli/exec-approvals-cli.ts`
- `src/cli/exec-policy-cli.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.ts`
- `src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts`
- `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`
- `src/node-host/invoke-system-run.ts`
- `src/node-host/invoke.ts`

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: stale allowlist metadata writeback no longer restores policy when the approvals file hash changed; the remaining reload-before-save race is covered by regression tests.
- Edge cases checked: empty/no-op metadata update, current hash mismatch, and changed-file lint/type/build behavior.
- What you did **not** verify: A full repo-wide `pnpm test` completed previously with baseline/no-output infrastructure noise in the submission workflow; after the final amended commit, verification was limited to targeted regression tests plus build/check/lint.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Approval metadata such as `lastUsedAt` may be skipped when an admin concurrently changes policy.
  - Mitigation: This is intentional fail-closed behavior for policy freshness. A later execution can record metadata once it evaluates the latest approvals snapshot.
- Risk: Locking approval writes changes local write sequencing.
  - Mitigation: The lock is scoped to exec approvals writes and validation passed through build, check, lint, and targeted regression tests.

target.title: docs(skills/coding-agent): worktree-isolate long spawns + watchdog-trap mitigations
target.body:
## Summary

Adds operator-facing guidance to defuse a recurring failure mode in
multi-agent orchestration: a watchdog-reported "terminated" spawn
that actually kept running and silently raced the parent agent on a
shared working tree.

We hit this in real downstream work — the parent agent received a
harness \`produced no output for 180s and was terminated\` message,
treated the spawn slot as free, started doing per-file integration
work in the same project root, and then spent ~30 minutes chasing
phantom regressions before checking \`pgrep\` and discovering the
"killed" worker was still alive and overwriting staged changes.

This PR is the operator-side workaround. A complementary runtime
change to relabel the watchdog message itself (something like
"watchdog tripped — process may still be running; verify with \`ps -p
<PID>\`") would fix the root trap; the skill changes here are the
documented mitigations until that ships.

## Changes (all in \`skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md\`)

1. **Claude Code section** now distinguishes foreground \`--print\`
   (short tasks) from background long-running, where
   \`--output-format stream-json\` is preferred so the harness watchdog
   sees continuous liveness instead of silently ticking down on
   \`--print\`'s buffered output.

2. **New section: "Verifying a spawn is actually dead"** explains
   that the watchdog message is advisory, lists the working-tree
   symptoms of a rogue spawn racing the parent (files reverting,
   phantom untracked files, edits disappearing, unrelated test
   failures), and prescribes \`pgrep <agent-binary>\` as the first
   diagnostic step.

3. **Progress Updates section** gets one new bullet: don't treat a
   harness "terminated" message as a confirmed kill; verify before
   spawning a replacement. Points readers at the new section.

4. **Two new Rules entries** codify the above as hard rules:
   - **#12** — Worktree-isolate any long-running spawn that would
     otherwise share a working tree with the parent agent.
   - **#13** — Watchdog "terminated" messages are advisory, not
     authoritative. Verify with \`ps -p\` / \`pgrep\`. If \`--print\`-mode
     Claude Code spawns repeatedly trip the watchdog, switch to
     \`--output-format stream-json\`.

## Why these specifically

- \`--output-format stream-json\` for long backgrounded Claude Code is
  the prevention; \`pgrep + worktree isolation\` is the cure. Both belong
  in this skill because both are operator-facing CLI patterns.
- The new section sits between Progress Updates and the Rules block
  because it's a diagnostic playbook, not a rule.
- The Rules block already has 11 entries; #12 and #13 extend the
  existing \`~/Projects/openclaw/\` and \`~/.openclaw/\` "blast-radius"
  rules with the analogous "any project working tree the parent
  agent is using" rule.

## Test plan

- [ ] Read the diff (62 lines added, 1 deleted; one file).
- [ ] Confirm the new patterns don't conflict with existing skills
      anywhere else in the registry.
- [ ] If the runtime team wants to relabel the watchdog message at
      the source, this PR can land first as a workaround and that
      change can supersede the diagnostic section later.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve unresolved service account refs
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve unresolved Google Chat `serviceAccountRef` as an error instead of silently falling back to legacy inline `serviceAccount` credentials.
- Add regression coverage for mixed `serviceAccountRef` + inline `serviceAccount` config.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Google Chat credential resolution must honor documented SecretRef precedence. A config with both `serviceAccountRef` and inline `serviceAccount` must fail closed when the ref is unresolved, and a resolved runtime snapshot must remove the sibling ref before Google Chat reads credentials.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree on the rebased head `ef2bfcd48f`, using Node/pnpm from the repo environment. I checked env and `~/.profile` for Google Chat service-account material without printing values; none was present, so this uses redacted service-account JSON through the production Google Chat account resolver plus runtime SecretRef assignment/resolution paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm exec tsx` against `extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts` and `extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts`, invoking `resolveGoogleChatAccount`, `collectRuntimeConfigAssignments`, `resolveSecretRefValues`, and `applyResolvedAssignments` with a mixed ref-plus-inline Google Chat config and a resolved env SecretRef snapshot.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the production-path proof command:

```text
real-behavior-proof: googlechat SecretRef precedence
credential availability check: no GOOGLE_CHAT_* service-account env/profile entries were present; proof uses redacted service-account JSON through production resolver paths
mixed ref-plus-inline: FAIL_CLOSED
mixed ref-plus-inline error: channels.googlechat.accounts.default.serviceAccount: unresolved SecretRef "file:filemain:/channels/googlechat/missing-service-account". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
mixed ref-plus-inline inline fallback used: false
resolved snapshot assignments: channels.googlechat.accounts.prod.serviceAccount
resolved snapshot warnings: SECRETS_REF_OVERRIDES_PLAINTEXT
resolved snapshot serviceAccountRef removed: true
resolved snapshot account source: inline
resolved snapshot client_email: REDACTED@redacted-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
resolved snapshot private_key: REDACTED
resolved snapshot inline legacy used: false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale mixed `serviceAccountRef` plus inline `serviceAccount` case failed closed before inline fallback, and the resolved snapshot path produced an account with the redacted resolved service-account email, removed `serviceAccountRef`, and did not use the legacy inline email.

**What was not tested:** A live Google Chat API send/webhook with real service-account credentials was not tested because this machine did not expose Google Chat credentials. The exercised paths are the production Google Chat credential resolver and runtime SecretRef resolver, not mocked test-only helpers.

## Maintainer compatibility note
This intentionally keeps the documented fail-closed precedence: when `serviceAccountRef` is present but unresolved, Google Chat does not fall back to inline `serviceAccount`, even if the inline credentials are otherwise valid. Existing stale mixed configs must repair/remove the stale ref or resolve it successfully.

## Tests
- `pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: feat(feishu): add segment streaming mode
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Feishu streaming delivery mode that accumulates model output and flushes complete segments at pause points, without changing the existing CardKit streaming-card default.

## Motivation

Feishu currently uses `channels.feishu.streaming` for CardKit live streaming. That behavior is useful when users want one live card that updates as the model generates text.

Some workflows need a different delivery style: show completed model text before a tool call, keep tool/status output in order, and then send the follow-up answer after the model resumes. Sending these boundaries as complete segments avoids sentence-by-sentence card updates while still giving users useful progress before the final reply.

## Configuration Behavior

`streaming` remains the top-level enable/disable switch for Feishu streaming delivery. `streamingMode` only applies when `streaming` is enabled.

| `streaming` | `streamingMode` | Behavior                                                                                                                                                |
| ----------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `false`     | `"card"`        | Streaming is disabled. Feishu sends replies through the regular non-streaming path; the mode value is ignored.                                          |
| `false`     | `"segment"`     | Streaming is disabled. Partial text is not accumulated and pause-point segment flushing is not enabled; the mode value is ignored.                      |
| `true`      | `"card"`        | Existing CardKit streaming behavior. Feishu updates one live card and closes it with the final content. This remains the default.                       |
| `true`      | `"segment"`     | New pause-point segment behavior. Feishu accumulates partial text and sends complete segments on tool start, idle, block, tool payload, or final reply. |

`chunkMode` still applies after a reply or segment is ready to send. In segment mode, `streamingMode: "segment"` decides when to flush a semantic segment, then `chunkMode` decides whether that segment needs to be split by length or newline before sending to Feishu.

## Changes

- Add `channels.feishu.streamingMode` with supported values:
  - `"card"`: default, existing CardKit streaming behavior
  - `"segment"`: accumulate model text and flush at pause points
- Preserve current `channels.feishu.streaming` semantics:
  - `true` enables streaming delivery
  - `false` disables streaming delivery
  - default mode remains CardKit streaming
- In segment mode:
  - partial model output is accumulated
  - accumulated text is flushed on tool start, idle, block, tool payload, or final reply
  - duplicate final text after an idle flush is suppressed
  - segment sends are serialized so final/tool payloads cannot overtake an in-flight partial flush
  - each flushed segment independently uses `renderMode: "auto"`
  - mentions are attached only to the first text segment
  - reasoning preview callbacks stay disabled because segment mode does not use live cards
- Update Feishu config schema, generated config metadata, docs, and tests.

## Example

Use segment delivery with:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    feishu: {
      streaming: true,
      streamingMode: "segment",
    },
  },
}
```

Default behavior remains:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    feishu: {
      streaming: true,
      streamingMode: "card",
    },
  },
}
```

## Compatibility

- Existing configs that only set `channels.feishu.streaming` keep the same behavior because `streamingMode` defaults to `"card"`.
- `streaming: false` continues to disable streaming delivery regardless of `streamingMode`.
- Segment mode is opt-in and does not change CardKit streaming-card behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu configs can opt into `streamingMode: "segment"`, and the dispatcher sends one completed segment before a tool pause and a second completed segment after the tool returns.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway connected to a private Feishu DM using the dev Feishu account, running the PR checkout `feishu-segment-streaming-mode-clean` at commit `ad678ae16e9`. Node.js `v22.22.2`; pnpm dependencies installed in `/root/.openclaw/workspace-dev/projects/fork-repos/openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent this Feishu DM to the dev agent: `First reply with exactly one line: segment-one-ok. Then you must call a tool to check the current date or time. After the tool returns, reply with exactly one line: segment-two-ok.` The dev Feishu account was configured with `streaming: true` and `streamingMode: "segment"`.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime log and copied live output are included below. Gateway journal shows `segment flush reason=toolStart` followed by `segment flush reason=final`; agent session output shows `segment-one-ok`, `toolCall: session_status`, and `segment-two-ok`.
- Observed result after fix: The live Feishu turn emitted `segment-one-ok`, paused for the `session_status` tool call, then emitted `segment-two-ok`. Gateway logs show two segment flushes for the same Feishu turn, first with `reason=toolStart` and then with `reason=final`, and the dispatch completed with `replies=2`.
- What was not tested: Broad multi-user/load behavior and media upload fallback in a real Feishu tenant were not tested in this live run; media fallback behavior is covered by the targeted Feishu dispatcher tests below.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, Feishu had no `streamingMode: "segment"` branch, so this live turn would use the existing CardKit streaming-card/default delivery path instead of two segment flushes.

Copied and redacted gateway journal plus agent-session output from the live Feishu run:

```text
# Redacted OpenClaw gateway journal, 2026-05-06 23:12 Asia/Shanghai
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: received message from <redacted_sender> in <redacted_chat> (p2p)
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: Feishu[dev-bot] DM from <redacted_sender>: First reply with exactly one line: segment-one-ok. Then you must call a tool to check the current date or time. After the tool returns, reply with exactly one line: segment-two-ok.
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: dispatching to agent (session=agent:dev:feishu:direct:<redacted_sender>)
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot] segment flush reason=toolStart mode=text chars=14
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot] segment flush reason=final mode=text chars=14
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=2)

# Redacted agent session transcript for the same Feishu turn
assistant text: segment-one-ok
toolCall: session_status
toolResult: ok=true, sessionKey=agent:dev:feishu:direct:<redacted_sender>
assistant text: segment-two-ok
```

Supplemental schema/metadata proof from the same checkout:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e 'import { FeishuConfigSchema } from "./extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts"; import { GENERATED_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_CONFIG_METADATA } from "./src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts"; const parsed = FeishuConfigSchema.parse({ streaming: true, streamingMode: "segment", accounts: { main: { streaming: true, streamingMode: "card" } } }); const feishu = GENERATED_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_CONFIG_METADATA.find((entry) => entry.channelId === "feishu"); console.log("top-level streamingMode:", parsed.streamingMode); console.log("account streamingMode:", parsed.accounts.main.streamingMode); console.log("metadata streamingMode enum:", feishu.schema.properties.streamingMode.enum.join(",")); console.log("account metadata streamingMode enum:", feishu.schema.properties.accounts.additionalProperties.properties.streamingMode.enum.join(","));'
top-level streamingMode: segment
account streamingMode: card
metadata streamingMode enum: card,segment
account metadata streamingMode enum: card,segment
```

## Tests

- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed, 3 test files / 102 tests
- `pnpm config:channels:check` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed

target.title: fix(chat-bash): inherit exec policy defaults
target.body:
## Summary
- repair #50392 so `/bash` chat sessions inherit the effective `tools.exec` defaults used by the exec tool
- include security, ask, path prepend, safe-bin settings/profiles, strict inline eval, approval running notice timing, timeout, and notification defaults where supported
- keep existing bash/elevated gates intact and avoid the bang-alias routing regression from #41598

## Credit
This repairs and carries forward the contributor work from @iron6909 in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/50392. It also preserves attribution for @updatedb's overlapping host/security/ask inheritance fix in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/41598.

## Review Notes
- Address Greptile findings on `execPolicy` consistency and `approvalRunningNoticeMs` forwarding.
- Address clawsweeper parity findings for the remaining exec defaults and focused regression coverage.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-157029-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/50392, https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/41598
- Credit: Primary repair branch is #50392 by @iron6909: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/50392.; Preserve source attribution for @updatedb's overlapping host/security/ask inheritance work in #41598: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/41598.; Do not carry forward #41598's commands-bash markdown-image routing change unless it is reworked to preserve punctuation-prefixed bang commands.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed
